text.content:
“The cards know more than they tell.” At the start of each chapter, Zellara — and, after the Old Fishery, her haunted deck — deals the party a Harrow reading.

What it is. A short narrative reading that foreshadows the chapter (a coming time of unrest; the PCs are fated heroes) and grants each PC a small luck boon. Read the omens; don't spoil concrete events.

How to run it. Deal each PC one card from a real Harrow deck (your own — we ship no card text), or use the Harrow Reading macro / the Harrow Reading — Suit Draw rolltable to assign a suit. Each card's suit maps to one ability score:

  • Hammers → Strength · Keys → Dexterity · Shields → Constitution · Books → Intelligence · Stars → Wisdom · Crowns → Charisma

The boon (CHG-0010 — hero-point-style). The reading grants each PC one Harrow Point for the chapter: spend it like a Hero Point to reroll a check or save that uses the card-suit's ability score (keep the new result). Unspent Harrow Points fade at the next reading.

Optional swap. Groups wanting a deeper subsystem can run the Stolen Fate Harrowing ritual instead; the suit-boon default above keeps it light.

Anchor item: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck} (recovered in the Old Fishery, inhabited by her spirit). It becomes the Harrow Deck of Many Things in Chapter 6. We ship the mechanics only; the 54 card meanings come from your own Harrow deck.

Chapter → suit (the lost sidebar). The print sidebar mapping each chapter to a suit didn't survive OCR — these journals are now the carrier. Each chapter's Harrowing pulls its Choosing from this suit, and Harrow Points that chapter reroll the matching ability:

ChapterSuitAbilityAlternate 1-point spends (free action)
1. Edge of AnarchyKeysDexterity+1 status AC or +10-ft Speed for one encounter
2. Seven Days to the GraveShieldsConstitutionresistance 3 to physical for one encounter; or after a fight, 1 minute's rest restores HP equal to your level
3. Escape from Old KorvosaBooksIntelligence+1 status to one spell's DC; or attempt one trained-only action untrained
4. A History of AshesHammersStrength+5 status damage with melee Strikes for one combat; or ignore an object's Hardness for 1 round
5. Skeletons of ScarwallStarsWisdomyour next vitality/void spell gains +1 to its DC and +1 die
6. Crown of FangsCrownsCharismaforce the GM to reroll one d20 (you live with the result)

The Chosen: each PC's Choosing card silently keys ONE encounter that chapter — when it begins, that PC gains +1 status to rolls the chapter's ability drives, for the encounter (don't reveal it in advance). Endgame: the deck transcends — the full 54-draw Harrow Deck of Many Things rolltable and procedure live in the 7. Appendices & Beyond journal.

Korvosa's many point-trackers and its five settlement stat blocks all map to three official PF2e subsystems (GM Core) — built once, reused from Ch.1 on.

Reputation (CHG-0013). Track the party's standing with Korvosa and each faction (Korvosan Guard, the Crown, Old Korvosa's bosses, the Shoanti, later the Gray Maidens). Deeds move Victory Points along a Reputation track (Ignored → Liked → Admired → Revered, or Disliked → Hated); standing gates favors, backup, prices, and access.

City tiers (CHG-0012). The five Korvosa settlement stat blocks become a Reputation- and chapter-state-driven item-availability table: as the city slides (anarchy → plague → martial law) and your Reputation shifts, the level and quantity of buyable items change. The framework is Reputation tier → available item level / purchase limit; set specifics per chapter.

Influence (CHG-0014). Court and social set-pieces (the doctors' soiree, dealing with Devargo, the war council, researching the queen) run as Influence encounters: each key NPC gets an Influence block — Influence DC, Discovery DCs, and thresholds → outcomes, with Resistances/Weaknesses. Built per-encounter in their chapters.

Victory Points (CHG-0013): Respect / Rebellion / faction trackers → VP clocks. Rumor Mill (CHG-0012): the Korvosan rumor table is shipped as a scaffold rolltable (Korvosa — Rumor Mill: original paraphrase hooks with truth flags; chapter slots 17-20 are yours to seed) — a few entries are plot hooks, most are flavor.

Korvosan Guard — ranks, gates & benefits (Appendix 6, converted). Cressida inducts the PCs in Ch.1 (Event 9). Rank advances only at the gates below — deeds, not points:

RankGateBenefits
R1Induction (Ch.1, Event 9)borrow up to 10 gp of gear per mission; +1 circumstance to Gather Information at guard posts
R2prove you're no crown agent — Trinia's escape (end Ch.1)borrow 50 gp; Recovery 1; free retraining access (Citadel trainers)
R3a great service to the citizens — ending blood veil (end Ch.2)borrow 100 gp; command a team (1d6 L3 guards, one mission); Recovery 2
R4a personal, risky stand against the queen — rescuing Neolandus (end Ch.3; accusation without proof doesn't count)borrow 250 gp; Recovery 3

Recovery 1: an overnight at Citadel Volshyenek (Ch.1–3) or the Dead Warrens (Ch.4–6) restores all HP and grants one counteract attempt (+15) against a curse, disease, or blindness/deafness — not available during Ch.2's plague peak. Recovery 2: adds restoration-grade care and a raise dead (components covered or defrayed by the borrow limit). Recovery 3: adds greater restoration-grade care and resurrection-grade returns.

City Tiers — the concrete numbers (CHG-0012, shipped). Korvosa's five settlement states, converted (PF1e marketplace ÷10). Restock: 1d4 lesser (≤L8) / 1d2 mid (L9–12) / 1 high (L13+) new items per month of play; a wanted specific item is in stock on a @Check[type:flat|dc:6] if at or under the tier's level cap.

TierTypical triggerItem level capSingle-purchase capHireling casting
Baseline (L14 city)Ch.1 startL148,500 gp7th rank
UnrestCh.1 riotsL115,000 gp5th rank
PlaguedCh.2 / Epidemic Clock ≥7L10 — disease-cure consumables need a @Check[type:flat|dc:11] to find at all5,000 gp5th rank
Martial LawCh.3–5 (curfew dusk–dawn)L8 lawful; black market L11 at +25% (@Check[type:underworld-lore|dc:25] to reach)2,500 gp3rd rank (the crown keeps 7th)
AnarchyCh.6 assault windowblack market only, L11 at +50% (@Check[type:underworld-lore|dc:20])5,000 gp5th rank

Allied NPC casters (Ishani, the Pharasmins, Thousand Bones) serve regardless of tier — but if the queen learns of one, expect an arrest and a rescue hook (the Deathhead Vault holds more than rebels).

Tie each PC to Korvosa, not just to Gaedren (CHG-0021). The AP's campaign traits convert to six PF2e custom backgrounds — each grants ability boosts, a trained skill, a Korvosa-themed Lore, and a recommended skill feat, with a built-in hook into the city. Every PC should also carry a personal grudge against Gaedren (the shared reason Zellara's reading draws them together).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgBetrayed000001]{Betrayed} — Deception + Underworld Lore
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgDrugAddict0001]{Drug Addict} — Medicine + Drug Lore
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgFramed00000001]{Framed} — Society + Korvosa Lore
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgLoveLost000001]{Love Lost} — Diplomacy + Korvosa Lore
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgMissingChild01]{Missing Child} — Survival + Korvosa Lore
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.bgUnhappyChild01]{Unhappy Childhood} — Thievery + Underworld Lore

Drag a background onto a PC to apply its boosts / trained skill / Lore; then add the recommended skill feat. Mechanics are ours (PF2e); the original trait flavor stays in your AP book.

Background goals → party XP (stated once, here). Each campaign background implies a personal goal: unmask the one who betrayed you · bury the addiction and its supplier · clear your framed name in law · lay your lost love to rest · recover the missing child from the Lambs · outgrow the unhappy childhood by facing its author. When a PC resolves their goal on-screen, award the whole party a 30 XP story award (or 80 XP if it closes a chapter villain or thread) — once per background. Gaedren's fall usually pays the first; later chapters echo the rest (the betrayer can resurface in Ch.3's Old Korvosa, the framed PC's exoneration rides Ch.3's seneschal, the child's fate can thread all the way to the Gray Maidens).

pages.name: Blood Veil — Persistent Plague & Epidemic Clock

text.content:
Blood Veil is the front end of Ileosa's blood harvest — the canon Blooding and the Everdawn Pool. The Chapter 2 cure suppresses the acute outbreak; the cult keeps re-seeding it at a slow burn, and it only truly ends when Ileosa falls (Ch.6). This is CHG-0011b (APPROVED, Major); it is removable at any chapter break.

Epidemic Clock (0–10) — a single campaign-state tracker. Set its Ch.2 start from the Survivor Count (more saved → lower). It advances +1 per in-city downtime week (and an extra +1 while the PCs are away in Ch.4/Ch.5), and recedes −1 when they act against it (distribute the suppressant, burn a cult cell, expose a Blooding site, fund the church). It drives the Korvosa city tier (see the Reputation page): ≥7 → Plagued; ≤3 → recovering.

ClockStateWhat the table feels
0–3RecedingFlavor only; markets recover; grateful NPCs — the PCs' wins show.
4–6SimmeringCheckpoints; some goods scarce; minor attitude drag; one personal hook (a known NPC falls ill).
7–8Spreading (Plagued)Harsher prices/availability; a district quarantined; lose an NPC or place; Guard support thins.
9Crisis (Martial Law)Riots/crackdowns; a district lost; a safe haven unusable; allies pressure the party.
10CatastrophePoint of no return: the Everdawn Pool gains charges early — the Ch.6 ritual timer shortens — and/or a mass-death beat darkens the denouement.

GM options menu (pick what hurts this table; telegraph one tier early): A PC infection (the disease below; Gritty/Heroic/Grim tones — always an out) · B lose an NPC (throwaway→named by tier) · C lose a place (safe house/shop/district, reversible) · D faction cost (Korvosan Guard / Abadar aid thins — ties to Reputation) · E accelerate the villain (shorter Ch.6 timer) · F moral pressure (plague vs. politics — no wrong answer).

Blood Veil (disease). @Check[type:fortitude|dc:24] for ordinary exposure, rising to @Check[type:fortitude|dc:30] for concentrated sources (the cult's brewing vats and raw fluids in Ch.2); on a critical failure the victim skips the onset and immediately suffers Stage 1. Onset 1 day. Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 day); Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day); Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day); Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3}. The PF1e Con+Cha damage becomes drained/enfeebled; the suppressant halts progression and steps the stage down rather than curing outright.

Persistent in-city exposure (atmosphere, not attrition): while in Plagued-tier Korvosa, a character attempts a flat check each downtime week — @Check[type:flat|dc:5] at a low clock, up to @Check[type:flat|dc:11] at a high clock — on a failure, save against Blood Veil at a reduced DC. Keep it light; make the clock respond to player action.

Track the clock + city tier on this page across chapters. Removability hatch: at any chapter break the GM may rule the suppressant became a true cure (revert to faithful) with zero downstream breakage. Full design: design/persistent_plague.md.

How this conversion handles the adventure's original prose — and why the journals you're reading may differ from another GM's build.

Injection, not inclusion. The build scripts never hardcode the adventure's prose (anchors of at most 8 words excepted). At build time, each read-aloud box, NPC speech, briefing, and player handout is pulled verbatim from the GM's own locally supplied AP text (the COTCT_AP_MD file). If that file is absent, every box falls back to an original paraphrase, so the module still works — it just reads in our words instead of Paizo's.

Player Handouts are explicitly exempt from the paraphrase rule, exactly like boxed read-aloud text: a handout's entire purpose is to be shown to players as written (Handouts #1-1/#1-2 in Chapter 1, #2-1/#2-2 in Chapter 2, and so on). They are injected from the GM's local source on the same terms as read-aloud boxes and are never shipped beyond the GM's own private build.

House rules of the build: every PF1e DC is re-judged to a PF2e @Check (never transliterated); valuables convert at roughly PF1e gp ÷ 10 while named magic items keep their identity as PF2e equivalents; XP story awards become milestone credit (CHG-0007). OCR artifacts in the source (dropped hyphens, two-column interleave) are repaired during injection — report any survivors as bugs.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 6 (Vencarlo Orisini / Becoming Blackjack) — pp.462-463.

Becoming Blackjack (subsystem). Blackjack is two hundred years of one mask — an identity handed to a single trainee per generation. Vencarlo Orisini is the current bearer; by campaign's end, a PC can be the next.

The kit (hidden in Vencarlo's bag of holding): mask, cloak, gloves, boots, daggers, and his mother's keen rapier. Finding it: discoverable in Ch.3 Part 1 (his ransacked home); otherwise Vencarlo approaches a chosen PC between Ch.3 and Ch.4. He prefers a private heir but accepts party-wide knowledge if all swear secrecy.

The mantle benefit: wearing the full kit and honoring the role, the bearer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception, Diplomacy, and Intimidation against Korvosan citizens of non-noble station (explicitly including Pilts Swastel's court) — the city believes in Blackjack harder than it believes in itself. Blackjack is a true second identity for impersonation and renown purposes.

The one-time rebuild: the inheritor may rebuild toward the role — reassign skill increases and feats (and, with GM blessing, class or subclass toward a swashbuckler/rogue silhouette). Deadline: the transition completes before the Deathhead Vault infiltration (Ch.4, Part 3). Vencarlo then retires (he keeps his stats; emergencies only). Ch.6 showcase: open the chapter with a beat built for the new Blackjack — the Trifaccia unmasking (Event 1) is tailor-made.

Module-authored checks, in the style of the community conversion documents. The Olliebird conversion is this module's DC authority wherever it speaks (384 rulings adopted verbatim, not repeated here); the 201 rulings below are the module's own — hazard Disable entries, unkeyed locks and climbs, subsystem ladders, and item riders. Basis tags in brackets name each DC's derivation. The full reference (with links to the community docs) lives in SUPPLEMENTAL_RULINGS.md.

Chapter 1: Edge of Anarchy

Part Three: Blood and Bones — All the World's Meat (B)

Part Three: Blood and Bones — The Dead Warrens (D)

Conversion Guide — Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock

  • Plagued-tier Korvosa: weekly downtime @Check[type:flat|dc:5] at a low Epidemic Clock up to DC 11 at a high clock; on a failure, save against Blood Veil at a reduced DC. [subsystem — Epidemic Clock (CHG-0011b)]

Notes

  • Counting convention: each skill+DC alternative counts as one check (e.g. '@Check[type:athletics|dc:16] or @Check[type:reflex|dc:16]' = 2); repeats of the same ruling at multiple placements are deduped (orphan rally appears 3x, C7 Diplomacy 24 2x, Devargo Fort 24 2x, B8/E5/E6 statblock-notes restatements of journal badges — each counted once).
  • Community-swapped docs skipped as dead fallbacks per the file's OLLIEBIRD ledger + community_index/aliases: 20 ch1 NPCs (Gaedren, Yargin, Hookshanks, Giggles, Drain Spider, Jigsaw Shark, Rioter, Amin, Grau, Trinia, Cow Hammer Boy, Verik, Enforcer, Devargo, Chittersnap, Skeletal Owlbear, Stirge, Cabbagehead, Vreeg) plus the Acid-Spraying Skulls hazard and Zellara's Harrow Deck item. Their builder action/save DCs (e.g. acid skulls Perception 25 / Thievery 25 / Reflex 22-basic; Trinia's Will 17 trio; Chittersnap's Reflex 21 / Fort 23; Vreeg's Fort 24 spell suite) never ship from the compendium packs and are not listed; the community exports' internal DCs are theirs.
  • Merged content on swapped docs IS live and was classified: actor publicNotes append as 'Conversion notes (this module)' (Amin E5 16/19, Grau E6 19/18, Verik B8 16/27, Chittersnap C15 Reflex 15 — all community rulings), and kept gear the community omitted (Devargo's venom Fort 24, Vreeg's Robe Occultism 22 — both listed as ours).
  • Blood Veil Fort DC 24 (ordinary) / 30 (concentrated) with crit-fail-skips-onset follows the community ch2.txt Conversion Guide band per the file's DC-alignment ledger — counted as community, not listed.
  • Restated standing rule (counted 2, excluded): the community's 'D2: @Check[type:perception|dc:21] to find the secret doors' is restated inline at D6 (secret door to D2) and on the kept Derro statblock's crawl-tunnel tactics.
  • Shingles Chase: the community doc only links a scribe.pf2.tools obstacle set (cH6jOT4J) with no local mirror in research/; the module's 15-obstacle table (30 DCs) is module-authored against the PF2e Chase subsystem and is listed as ours. If the GM can pull the scribe doc, those 30 DCs should be re-audited against it.
  • Knivesies (C7) is a module-authored mini-game using opposed Athletics Shove/Trip and Interact actions — no fixed DCs, so no entries; same for the C7 bribe ladder (~+1 attitude per 10 gp) and the Harrow Point subsystem (rerolls, no DCs). Influence DCs (CHG-0014) are deferred to per-chapter builds with no values shipped in ch1.
  • Hazard chassis numbers that are not rollable player DCs (boardwalk/deck hardness-HP, door Hardness 5 / HP figures) excluded per task scope, as are @Damage entries.
  • Quirk noticed in passing (no edit made — read-only task): build_pilot.py's AW()/HW() append pre-swap copies to the Adventure bundle lists BEFORE B.write() applies the community swap, so packs/adventure/_source/cotct-edge-of-anarchy.json embeds OUR statblocks (e.g. Trinia with Wand of Daze Monster) while packs/actors ships the community versions (Wand of Color Spray). Adventure-import users would see the dead-fallback DCs after all; worth a follow-up fix.

Chapter 2: Seven Days to the Grave

Community rulings adopted: 76 · module-authored: 21.

Part One: Infection — Yellow Lights

  • Yellow Lights: DC 10/15/20 Diplomacy to Gather Information about the sunken ship (rumor ladder — higher results are truer). [simple DC — untrained/trained/expert]

Part Two: Outbreak — Mission 2: Plague Rats (B)

  • B3/B6: @Check[type:acrobatics|dc:20] for a Medium creature to Squeeze through the wall-hole between the dens (Small creatures slip through freely; community silent — AP gates the gap to Small). [level-based — L5 mission Squeeze]
  • B4: @Check[type:athletics|dc:26] to lift either rusted iron grate penning the otyugh (Hardness 10, HP 60; jammed east winch retracts it after a minute's cranking). [GM Core — Force Open, stuck iron portal]

Part Two: Outbreak — Mission 3: The Color of Death (C)

  • C3: @Check[type:athletics|dc:24] to break open the boarded-up laboratory apartment (Hardness 5, HP 15; boarded inside and out). [level-based — L6 mission Force Open]

Part Two: Outbreak — Mission 4: The Hungry Dead (D)

  • D2: @Check[type:perception|dc:15] to find Giotorri's key ring on his desiccated corpse (opens D3 above). [simple DC — trained]
